title: Linking/Syncing status
excerpt: I use a workflow where if in one board a ticket reaches a certain status, it creates an item in another.  I would love a way to sync two status’ together so it updates both tickets when one is updated

posts.author: mtvanc (MT)
posts.cookedHtml: I use a workflow where if in one board a ticket reaches a certain status, it creates an item in another. I would love a way to sync two status’ together so it updates both tickets when one is updated

posts.author: Julia-monday.com (Julia Fagelman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @mtvanc

We have an automation in beta which could work for you:

You can activate it from monday labs in your avatar. Let me know if it solves your needs?

posts.author: mtvanc (MT)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @Julia-monday.com I enabled beta views, boards and widgets but can’t find it. Are you sure it’s enabled on our account?

posts.cookedHtml: would love to use this too @Julia-monday.com but don’t see it in the automation centre.

posts.author: ivan (ivan unwin)

posts.author: Eltjo (Eltjo Smit)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey Julia! I would love to be able to test this feature. Can you activate it for us as wel? Thanks!

posts.author: cbparrett (Christopher Parrett)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Julia-monday.com - Is there a version of this that occurs once a status is changed? For example we have a bunch of clients that require specific tasks. Once a task on one board is switched to a status we would like to have a copy of it made on one centralized board that shares a status column.

That way we can have all tasks of one type on a central board.

posts.author: ivan (ivan unwin)
posts.cookedHtml: could you add this to my account please

posts.author: abiciea (Mike)
posts.cookedHtml: yes, we require the same!

posts.author: Julia-monday.com (Julia Fagelman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all

This has now been released to everyone so should automatically appear in the automations centre!

posts.author: cbparrett (Christopher Parrett)
posts.cookedHtml: Any chance on the variant that is driven by status?

posts.author: kevin (Kevin)
posts.cookedHtml: Just chiming in – I could also really benefit from having an automation where a status change on an item in board A triggers an identical status change on board B.

posts.author: JordanD (Jordan Di Conti)
posts.cookedHtml: A similar automation that does the reverse would be great. One that creates the link on the second board rather than on the original would help us out a lot. We are currently manually linking secondary boards to a main one. Probably our biggest pain point so far with the product and the number 1 thing preventing us from expanding monday company wide.

posts.author: cbparrett (Christopher Parrett)
posts.cookedHtml: Can we keep this thread up for the variant request?

posts.author: abiciea (Mike)
posts.cookedHtml: Us as well! One of the Monday.com support members told me as far as they know there is no plan on adding this.

posts.author: JordanD (Jordan Di Conti)
posts.cookedHtml:
Very disappointing if true.

It seems odd and I can’t really wrap my head around the current automation. It seems completely backwards to me.

If I am using a main or master board why would I want to add an item to another board and then have mirrors from that board on my master board. I’d want the mirror columns on the sub board and not on the master or main board. It is backwards in the hierarchy of the boards.

posts.author: abiciea (Mike)
posts.cookedHtml: Agreed! I believe their reasoning is to avoid becoming a database platform. When you add two way sync, in theory, Monday.com would become become a relational database. I’m sure this would bring an influx of bugs and user questions that they are not ready to handle. I this is why, I can understand why hold off.

posts.author: cfraticelli (Celene Fraticelli)
posts.cookedHtml: This makes so much sense and unfortunately, is exactly what my teams needs

posts.author: Juhly02 (Ana Julia)
posts.cookedHtml: In my view, this is not an issue, it is an opportunity for Monday. The customer can choose the way of use… I am corssing fingers to see that feature soon! xD

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi All,

Are you looking to sync a status of a single item across two boards? That is not possible in monday.com but if I understand the exact requirement I can probably build something. I also build master - detail relationship recipes for reporting and these could probably be extended. See also the app annaouncement here Monday app with automations released - #4 by basdebruin

posts.author: kwulster (Kathryn Wulster)
posts.cookedHtml: We would love to have this functionality (updating a status in one board updates the status of a linked item in another board). Can you please let me know what would be required to move forward?

posts.author: InesCV (Ines Castelltort)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @JustinJ, is it possible to enable this beta feature in my boards too. It would be really helpful! Thank you very much!

posts.author: artnic (Rafael)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi, all.

I ended up here because I have the same need.

I tried to enable this in monday.labs but it seems I don’t have access.

Monday Support, can you help me?

Thanks in advice.

posts.author: Cris (Cristina)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello, can I have access to this automation. It does not show on the automations center. TY

posts.author: IDavies (Ian Davies)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,
I am looking for something which can’t figure out. I have created a task in one board; used the automation - create item in new board when status set to Open.
Now I want the statuses to be linked, so if I change the status on board 1 it is reflected in the task created on board 2 and if vice versa. I dont want to have to change the status on each item in both boards individually.
Any suggestions?

posts.author: Nperez (Nick)
posts.cookedHtml: I’d like access to this as well. I can’t find it on labs. Thank you!

posts.author: JackiSto (Jacki Stockinger)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m in the same boat - I have connected items across two boards and want a two-way sync of a status column. Fully expected that this would be existing functionality… but I’m brand new to Monday and still discovering.

posts.author: kphays (Katelyn Hays)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’ve successfully linked statuses between two boards using the mirror column, but it can get overwhelming when there are multiple mirror columns and/or linked boards to one board.

We have a board we use as a team board and several team members use individual boards for their work but sync up to the team board when items are created on the individual boards.

I’ve also tried the beta automation that links board columns for status changes by “logic” but it doesn’t update to the matching item in the other board, it puts the updates on random items instead.
